response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack/web software craftsman but lacks the core domain expertise required for a Principal Performance Architect role in AI accelerator hardware. No SoC/IP modeling, no hardware-software co-design, no deep neural network optimization, no GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture experience. The resume shows web, mobile, and backend work — not silicon performance architecture. Educational path (BS + ~14 years experience) technically meets the minimum bar, but the technical skill gap is too large to close.
strengths: Long tenure (14+ years) across diverse stacks including low-latency D systems at Dunnhumby
Some embedded and C/C++ exposure at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, testing, performance-aware backend work
concerns: No SoC, IP modeling, or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or DNN training/inference optimization background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack, not silicon or systems-level performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Missing AI accelerator / SoC modeling expertise
No GPU / SIMD / MIMD / DNN optimization background
